entry.title: The Raise of NoCode/ Low Code Developers
entry.content:
Low code No code developers have become new personas who could influence the business in achieving the desired business transformation with

·      Speed up the development

·      Adapt to change

·      Reduced learning curve

As per Gartner report the low-code/no-code (LCNC) market has reached $26.9 billion in 2023, a 20% increase from 2022. While Forrester estimates it will be worth $45.5 billion by 2025.

 

Why do we feel the Low Code/No code is on the Raise

Low-code/No-code platforms allow businesses to quickly build and deploy applications without the need for expensive and time-consuming coding projects. This helps businesses adapt to market changes and transform rapidly based on customer needs, which is essential for digital transformation.

Low-code tools are also expected to integrate more with technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), and the Internet of Things (IoT), making these complex technologies more accessible to non-technical users and leveraging Business knowledge to help in better business transformations.

 

How it works

Low-code/No-code tools provide Templates. Many processes in your organization can include inefficiencies and bottlenecks leading to repetitive and boring activities, these tasks could be automated templatized, and made as readily usable content to improvise your organization's business process

These tools also provide features like simple intuitive Drag-and-drop interfaces enabling business users to put their ideas to action. With embedded AI/ML we could also support the business/novice user to reuse and/or create automation with all the contextual data/information.

Low code/No code Tools/Platforms should also provide the Governance mechanism needed to have a tap on the showdown IT which could lead to resource exploitation.

 

Thus No-code and low-code development platforms provide potential options for reducing software complexity, time, and cost. With generative AI we will also be enabling the business users/ Novice developers to add value to the system in a very pragmatic manner. However, having a stringent governance process would become the need of the hour to avoid the risks associated with shadow IT like compliance violations, resource exploitation, cost and Inefficiencies, etc.

 

#SAPBuild #LCNC #slithoughtleadership #SLIThoughtLeadership

entry.title: Enterprise Automation: Deep Dive into the new Generative AI capabilities!
entry.content:
After the introduction to the topic by Thomas Volmering, I would be happy to support you with the deep dive into the AI capabilities of Enterprise Automation in this blog post.

  1. SAP Signavio which provide visibility into business processes, potential bottlenecks, root-cause analysis, and suggestions for improvement.
  2. SAP Build Process Automation which automates workflow processes and tasks with drag-and-drop simplicity using pre-built workflows, bots and connectors for SAP and non-SAP systems.
  3. SAP Integration Suite which helps to seamlessly integrate diverse capabilities available in complex IT landscapes with proven pre-built integrations, APIs, connectors, and best practices.

Generative AI in SAP Signavio

In order to leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) technologies for business processes, we introduced the concept of Large Process Models (LPM). This new concept was presented last year by Dr. Andreas Gerber, VP Head of Engineering SAP Signavio Process AI.  The idea is to go far beyond the traditional LLMs  by finetuning them and augmenting them to focus on their application in the area of business processes.  

For that, Large Process Models are combining generic LLM AI technologies with the know-how of decades of process experience around SAP from thousands of experts. They are trained with anonymized customer data from various customers and refined with domain- or industry-specific fine-tuning, and customer-specific fine-tuning. This broad and diverse training set helps ensure that our process AI capabilities are uniquely equipped to tackle real-world business and process challenges with precision, drawing on deep industry knowledge and process understanding. 

Our LPM-powered process AI capabilities have the exceptional ability to answer your specific process questions and help you uncover issues you were not aware of. They act as your trusted advisor, providing you with ready-to-consume AI-powered recommendations you can apply right away or use as a foundation to drive process enhancements. 

Upcoming capabilities in SAP Signavio powered by GenAI  leveraging Large Process Models will be:

Process Recommender 

Our AI-assisted process recommender ​will provide process and domain owners with the ability to obtain preconfigured process models that are a best match to their needs, based on a database of 5,000 best practices. ​

With this capability, you will narrow down thousands of options to those process models that best suit the needs of your organization. ​

Recommendations are grounded in years of SAP experience working side by side with industry leaders and thousands of projects delivered. ​

Planned for Q3 2024, Process Recommender will bring key benefits such as: ​

  • Instant recommendations based on best practices ​
  • Accelerated process model design ​
  • No need for consulting-heavy services to define the first process draft to start the modeling activity

 

Performance Indicators Recommender

Our AI-assisted Performance Indicators Recommender ​capability will provide process owners and process analysts instant recommendations on the process performance indicators to be monitored.

Users can move from problem identification to a measurement approach with no effort by leveraging a repository of thousands KPIs and PPIs

Key benefits of Performance Indicators Recommender, to be available in Q3 2024, include: ​

  • Instant recommendations based on best practices ​
  • Simple connection between business problem, affected process and relevant metric to be measured
  •  Self-service approach to define initial process monitoring framework

SAP Signavio Process Analyzer

 Our AI-assisted Process Analyzer, empowers users with any skillset to ask questions in natural language and receive immediate insights that can inform business decisions.

Key Benefits ​of this new module, scheduled for Q4 2024, include:

  • Correlation and anomaly detection easily consumable by business users ​
  • Democratized process mining ​
  • Accuracy and reliability and relevance of the analysis output based on business user prompts  ​

 

 

Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation, with Executable Process AI

Our aim for Process Automation is to go further, and we now want to provide Executable Process AI and more.

Indeed, there are challenges when automating processes:

  • No active assistance to business experts while starting automation of critical processes
  • Slow pace of incrementing large process automation​
  • Difficulty to comprehend already modelled artifacts when lacking adequate documentation

To tackle these challenges, we decided that SAP Build Process Automation should include

  • Conversational AI capabilities within the studio
  • Semantic data access and knowledge graph access within automation and process design time
  • Auto-generation of automations and process segments based on embedded intelligence within the studio

6 months ago, at SAP TechEd 2023, we unveiled a first demo of Joule copilot creating the workflow for an approval process. You can see in this video a preview of how generative AI will help creating a new process workflow automation, with a business rule based on a decision table for approval.

Our solution, SAP Build Process Automation, will soon bring embedded generative AI capabilities to generate processes, automations, and decisions, including:

  • Interactive Model Generation and Modifications
  • Summarization of Complex Artifacts in natural language

With Artifacts Generation and Modification, you will be able to describe your process in natural language, interact and iterate to finalize desired process flow and finally generate the process that fits your requirement. It will generate processes, automations, forms, and decisions (business policies) using the inputs provided by business expert in natural language. And of course, generated artifacts which can be reviewed and modified as per need.

Summarization will create a short or comprehensive description of complex historical or newly generated complex artifacts, for instance the summary of a complex decision table. You will be able to share or save the description along with the artifact.

In a few words, SAP Build Process Automation will bring tremendous benefits to our customers, by

  • offering a conversational experience for citizen developers
  • accelerating time to value for authoring automations and processes
  • standardizing SAP application access for automations through a semantic access layer fostering reuse and fail-safe connectivity

These evolutions are planned for Q3 2024, and you can discover all the improvements planned in Process Automation by browsing SAP Build Process Automation public roadmap.

 

Generative AI in SAP Integration Suite

Integration, the foundation of an intelligent enterprise, is becoming the number one strategic priority for organizations. Only by connecting customer, product, employee, and brand experiences with business operations, continuously and in real time, can organizations exploit the full potential of their system landscape and make the right decision at the right time. With SAP Integration Suite, you can integrate solutions inside and outside your data center. You can accelerate connectivity with prebuilt integration flows, connectors to hundreds of systems, thousands of APIs, and other capabilities that simplify integrations with SAP and third-party applications. As shown in the figure, connecting people in real time becomes a reality with event- driven architectures and intelligent technology that helps you automate comprehensive business processes to gain efficiency and visibility.

There are a few AI-based capabilities in SAP Integration Suite already, such as a conversational assistant in SAP Business Accelerator Hub (beta), flow step recommendations, and recommendations for interfaces and mappings.

Now we are working to further integrate AI for 2 purposes:

  • Generation of Integration
  • Anomaly Detection

Generation of Integration

Our customers are facing complex integration challenges with the diverse applications and systems in their landscape. Integrators must have the right domain & technical expertise and invest a lot of time and effort to design new integrations & APIs.

Our solution is to leverage AI to generate Integrations: SAP Integration Site will be able to create new integration flows, based on the specific integration scenarios, different connecting applications, and business partners. It will leveraging Unified Customer Landscape – UCL - to discover the customer landscape and offer seamless integrations between those systems, and provide pre-configured connectors and scheduler.

 

Anomaly Detection

It is important to monitor deviations from the normal usage or performance in the API traffic, as it indicates security threats, system malfunctions or other issues that hinder developer & user experience.

Empowering our customers to maintain security, reliability & performance of their APIs, we will leverage AI for anomaly detection, identifying unusual or abnormal behaviors by continuously monitoring the API traffic. This AI-based mechanism will immediately notify API owners upon detection of anomalies in API Call Volume, API Error count, API Response time, while providing insights to aid investigate the cause & analyze impact and recommend remedial actions.

Watch the demo of Integration Suite new AI capabilities during Juergen Mueller's Innovation Keynote at SAP Sapphire 2024:

Click on the image to watch the video.

Thus, SAP Integration Suite will enable organizations to deliver better services & experience and hence achieve their business goals.

 

Demos of advanced Enterprise Automation at SAP Sapphire 2024

On June 4th at SAP Sapphire 2024, SAP CTO Juergen Mueller showcased an end-to-end demo of Enterprise Automation, in the context of extending SAP ERP in compliance with our Clean Core strategy:

Click on the image to watch the video.

First, new SAP Signavio Benchmarking Analytics presents a comprehensive dashboard of the business processes, called Process Performance Overview, which shows that the Lead to Cash process does not perform as well as it could. In particular, the analysis shows that many sales orders involve a manual step because delivery block is not removed after a customer pays an overdue bill.

Second, by using the new Build Extension Wizard embedded in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, one creates a new extension project:  a Business Event, occurring when a Journal Entry is created, triggers an automation created with SAP Build Process Automation which automatically removes the delivery block after the overdue bill is finally paid by the customer. That’s it!

Now, let us look into the other business scenario in more details: how can enterprise automation support us in intelligent processing of blocked sales orders.

This demo features the incremental value of all three components within the enterprise automation portfolio and highlights the latest advancements and innovative AI-based features that were added to SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Signavio, and SAP Integration Suite.

We start this use case with SAP Signavio Process Insights, where we can evaluate the entire process landscape and identify inefficiencies in the lead-to-cash line of business. We continue with a deep dive to the processes to identify the highest improvement potential. This analysis is supported by SAP Signavio Process AI, which is being used for intelligent language prompts, allowing us to find the right BPMN model and search for KPIs.

Next, we receive the recommendation that we have a very low automation rate for processing sales order documents that have a billing block attached to it. To solve the problem and automate the process, we jump to the SAP S/4HANA Extension Wizard, allowing us to create needed extensions, in this case to define and initiate a process in SAP Build.

Event Mesh service from the integration suite is being used to facilitate the event-based communications.

In SAP Build Process Automation, we can leverage executable process AI capability, allowing us to design the needed approval flow for the sales order. In this scenario, we will define a new business rule to process sales orders differently, which are exceeding a certain order amount. We can also use the executable process AI functionality to ask for a process summary.

With the help of this, we will be able to remove the billing block and then unblock the sales order.

Next, we can also use actions to trigger integration flows in SAP Integration Suite. The extensive library of connectors and adapters allows us to replicate sales order information into any SAP or third-party application, that is required. In this case, we use the embedded AI functionality to describe the integration that we need, which is going to be generated fully automatically. In this case, this integration flow will be used to replicate the sales order document via a SOAP adapter.

On top of that, with the AI-based anomaly detection in API management, we can also detect failures and analyze the entire API traffic to ensure operational excellence.

Finally, advanced event mesh takes care of high-volume event handling in multiple regions.

So, as you can see, Enterprise Automation suite with SAP Signavio, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Integration Suite is now more powerful than ever. Up to you now to harness this power to improve the efficiency of your business!

entry.title: Harness the Power of Generative AI for Enterprise Automation!
entry.content:
In a previous blog post, I presented the benefits of Enterprise Automation through the success stories from two very different customers, Blue Diamond Growers and Homemade. This gave you a first glimpse of how Enterprise Automation can quickly increase business efficiency in any organization.

Demo of advanced Enterprise Automation at SAP Sapphire 2024

At SAP Sapphire 2024, SAP CTO Juergen Mueller showcased a demo of Enterprise Automation, in the context of extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud in compliance with SAP’s Clean Core strategy:


Click the image to watch the Enterprise Automation demo

First, new SAP Signavio Benchmarking Analytics presents a comprehensive dashboard of the business processes, called Process Performance Overview, which shows that the Lead to Cash process does not perform as well as it could. In particular, the analysis shows that many sales orders involve a manual step because delivery block is not removed after a customer pays an overdue bill.

Second, by using the upcoming new Build Extension Wizard embedded in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, one creates a new extension project:  a Business Event, occurring when a Journal Entry is created, triggers an automation created with SAP Build Process Automation which automatically removes the delivery block after the overdue bill is finally paid by the customer.

That’s it!

The Future of Enterprise Automation is AI-driven

You’ve read that our customer Homemade is using an SAP Business AI service named Document Information Extraction to read documents and automatically enter the data captured from those documents into their systems. This is a very common use case for applying AI to business processes that many of our customers are already using. And we know that we can go much further in process automation by using Generative AI. That's why we've been working hard for several months to add GenAI capabilities to the 3 pillars that make up our Enterprise Automation suite:

  1. SAP Signavio which provide visibility into business processes, potential bottlenecks, root-cause analysis, and suggestions for improvement.
  2. SAP Build Process Automation which automates workflow processes and tasks with drag-and-drop simplicity using pre-built workflows, bot automations and connectors for SAP and non-SAP systems.
  3. SAP Integration Suite which helps to seamlessly integrate diverse capabilities available in complex IT landscapeswith proven pre-built integrations, APIs, connectors, and best practices.

I’m happy to share today an overview of this new capabilities powered by Generative AI.

For a comprehensive deep dive, I engage you to read Evgeniy Gorbunov’s blog post “Enterprise Automation: Deep Dive on new Generative AI capabilities”. 💡

Generative AI in SAP Signavio

To efficiently leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) technologies for business processes, our colleagues from SAP Signavio introduced last year the concept of Large Process Models (LPM). The idea is to go far beyond the traditional LLMs by finetuning them and augmenting them to focus on their application in the area of business processes. 

 Read more in the blog post written by Dr. Andreas Gerber, VP Head of Engineering SAP Signavio Process AI: “Large Process Models: Process Management in the Age of Generative AI”.

Upcoming capabilities in SAP Signavio powered by GenAI leveraging Large Process Models will be:

  • Process Recommender: our AI-assisted process recommender ​will provide process and domain owners with the ability to obtain preconfigured process models that are a best match to their needs, based on a database of 5,000 best practices. With this capability, you will narrow down thousands of options to those process models that best suit the needs of your organization. ​
  • Performance Indicators Recommender:  this ​capability will provide process owners and process analysts instant recommendations on the process performance indicators to be monitored. Users can move from problem identification to a measurement approach with no effort by leveraging a repository of thousands KPIs and PPIs
  • Process Analyzer: this capability empowers users with any skillset to ask questions in natural language and receive immediate insights that can inform business decisions.

Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation, with Executable Process AI

Our aim for Process Automation is to go further, and we now want to provide Executable Process AI and more. Indeed, there are challenges when automating processes:

  • No active assistance to business experts while starting automation of critical processes
  • Slow pace of incrementing large process automation​
  • Difficulty to comprehend already modelled artifacts when lacking adequate documentation

To tackle these challenges, we decided that SAP Build Process Automation should include

  • Conversational AI capabilities within the studio
  • Auto-generation of automations and process segments based on embedded intelligence within the studio

Our solution, SAP Build Process Automation, will soon bring embedded generative AI capabilities to generate processes, automations, and decisions, including:

  • Interactive Process Generation and Modifications: you will be able to describe your process in natural language, to interact and iterate to finalize desired process flow and finally generate the process with automations, forms, and decisions (business policies) that fits your requirement.

 

  • Summarization of Complex Artifacts: this capability will create a short comprehensive description of complex historical or newly generated complex artifacts, for instance the summary of a complex decision table.

In a few words, SAP Build Process Automation will bring tremendous benefits to our customers, by

  • offering a conversational experience for citizen developers
  • accelerating time to value for authoring automations and processes
  • standardizing SAP application access for automations through a semantic access layer fostering reuse and fail-safe connectivity

These evolutions are planned to be available in 2024, and you can discover all the improvements planned in Process Automation by browsing SAP Build Process Automation public roadmap.

Generative AI in SAP Integration Suite

Integration is the foundation of an intelligent enterprise: only by connecting customers, products, employees and brand experiences with business operations, continuously and in real time, can organizations exploit the full potential of their system landscape and make the right decision at the right time.

With SAP Integration Suite, you can integrate solutions inside and outside your data center. You can accelerate connectivity with prebuilt integration flows, connectors to hundreds of systems, thousands of APIs, and other capabilities that simplify integrations with SAP and third-party applications. There are a few AI-based capabilities in SAP Integration Suite already, such as a conversational assistant in SAP Business Accelerator Hub (beta), flow step recommendations, and recommendations for interfaces and mappings.

Now we are working to further integrate AI for 2 purposes:

  • Generation of Integration: this new capability can create new integration flows, based on the specific integration scenarios, different connecting applications, and business partners. It will be leveraging Unified Customer Landscape – UCL - to discover the customer landscape and offer seamless integrations between those systems and provide pre-configured connectors and scheduler.
  • Anomaly Detection: we will leverage AI for anomaly detection, identifying unusual or abnormal behaviors by continuously monitoring the API traffic. This AI-based mechanism will immediately notify API owners upon detection of anomalies in API Call Volume, API Error count, API Response time, while providing insights to aid investigate the cause & analyze impact and recommend remedial actions.

So, as you can see, Enterprise Automation suite with SAP Signavio, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Integration Suite will be more powerful than ever. Up to you now to harness this power to improve the efficiency of your business!

One more thing? Joule Extensibility in SAP Build!

In addition to Enterprise Automation, there is another amazing perspective involving SAP Build that I’m very proud to share with you: indeed, SAP Build will be the go-to low code extension layer for Joule, enabling customers to extend Joule by creating custom skills using advanced GenAI capabilities.​

Here is the announcement of Joule Extensibility with a demo shown by Juergen Mueller in his Innovation keynote:


Click the image to view the Joule extensibility demo

The benefits of Joule Extensibility through SAP Build for our customers and partners will be to

  • Provide support to business users in ad-hoc scenarios
  • Enhance collaboration and innovation across the SAP ecosystem
  • Reduce redundancy and leverage collective expertise to enhance business processes
  • Streamline the integration process, saving time and resources
  • To let customers stay ahead of the curve with access to the latest AI capabilities

Stay tuned to see how Joule and SAP Business AI can help you leverage artificial intelligence to improve your business efficiency!

entry.title: Summer spark: Inside SAP’s best moments and what’s coming…
entry.content:
As we wrap up the first half of the Inside SAP YouTube series, it's a perfect time to reflect on the insightful sessions we've had so far. Crafted and presented by SAP IT experts themselves, the series has been giving the inside scoop, guaranteeing real, in-depth insights into how SAP operates and its invaluable expertise in Enterprise Architecture, application development on SAP Business Technology Platform, and more.

Kicking off with the Senior Director of SAP Global Experience Technology, Oliver Gutzeit, the session provided a deep dive into the inner workings of SAP's digital landscape. With incredible insights from Christian Freytag and Alexandra Hofmann, we've learned how our digital IT organization supports our business as we evolve our enterprise architecture strategy, the implementation of SAP BTP as well as the SAP Build solutions. Each episode has showcased practical applications, hands-on demos, and innovative solutions, keeping the SAP community engaged and informed. The momentum from these sessions, starting in May, continuing after Sapphire and through the hot summer months, has been truly remarkable.

But wait, there’s more!

We will continue with the series in September, with our Chief Analytics Officer, Pinar Doelen. Throughout the next two sessions, you’ll explore SAP’s data-as-a-product approach, the role of SAP Datasphere, and how AI speeds up insights, as well as learn how SAP is transforming analytics with natural-language experiences in SAP Analytics Cloud.

We want to continue shining a light on SAP’s uniqueness, highlighting the capabilities and possibilities of our technology and solutions. Stay tuned as we return with even more exciting content after the break. In the meantime, you can watch the session replays to catch up on any valuable insights you might have missed. Visit the Inside SAP webpage, register for the series and join our sessions!

See you very soon!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, August 1st, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/08/01/sap-developer-news-august-1st-2024/

DESCRIPTION

SAP BTP Innobytes July edition

  • Video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CAVl-_HWHg8

August Developer Challenge

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/sap-developer-challenge-abap-core-data-services/ba-p/13778288

SAP TechEd Virtual Registration is Open!!

  • Registration Site: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

Major UX Update of Joule in SAP Build Code

  • Blog Post with Details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/major-ux-update-of-joule-in-sap-build-code/ba-p/13770686

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP BTP Innobytes July edition

2:09 August Developer Challenge

3:04 SAP TechEd Virtual Registration is Open!!

4:33 Major UX Update of Joule in SAP Build Code

TRANSCRIPT

[DJ] The July edition of SAP BTP InnoBytes is out now. If you didn't know already, you should know SAP BTP InnoBytes is a monthly series of videos bringing you snackable, up-to-date information about innovations on SAP BTP. This month we see items on security and identity and also on the SAP Task Center. So first of all the SAP Secure Login Service for SAPGUI has some enhancements allowing you to connect to and use customer managed certificate authorities. There's support already for AWS private CAs with more options in the roadmap. With SAP Cloud Identity Services, there are some enhancements to allow you to define user group assignments at the application level on top of the already existing user profile and tenant balance. And finally, with the SAP Task Center there are some innovations allowing you now to bring in and use task providers from non-SAP and legacy systems, bringing a much asked for unified task view. Find out more about these innovations in the InnoBytes July edition. Link is in the description. And, by the way, it's the end of July, just starting August, which means that the Reverse APIs challenge is now coming to an end. You can still work through the tasks, but we've got an amazing challenge coming up for you for the month of August, and I'm going to hand over to my lovely friend and colleague Shilpa to tell you all about it. Shilpa, over to you.

[Shilpa] Thanks, DJ, in passing the SAP Developer Challenge. I must admit, you kept the SAP Community hungry in releasing eleven tasks in your challenge. Me and Sheena will try to keep up the legacy in making an interesting August Developer Challenge. So with that, hello, everyone. you might have seen the ongoing SAP TechByte series on ABAP Core Data Services. So with that, it's a good idea to have the developer challenge related to ABAP CDS. Watch out for every Wednesday where a new task will be released. There's a prerequisite which will help you to get started with this challenge. Now this blog post gives the information about the challenge, quick links, health document, and the timeline as and when the task is updated. Check out the blog post, make sure to complete the prerequisite, and be ready for the challenge.

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here. Have you heard? Registration for SAP TechEd Virtua 2024 is now open, and I'm not the only one that gets excited about this time of year. My best friend Sully also loves TechEd time, and he's gearing up as well. SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 will be packed with a ton of information for architects and developers, ranging from lectures and jumpstart sessions, from voice of the partner sessions to keynotes, and of course your very own developer keynote, which we are changing up a bit this year and having more of a deconstructed approach to the developer keynote. So stay tuned for more details on that. So make sure to sign up for SAP TechEd Virtual which takes place on October 8th and 9th and also stay tuned for more information around the SAP TechEd on tour stops starting in November. And lastly don't forget about Devtoberfest which starts two weeks prior to the start of SAP TechEd Virtual and picks up or picks back up for an additional two weeks after the SAP TechEd virtual event and as always your Developer Advocate team will be playing a huge role in both Devtoberfest and SAP TechEd in general and we are all really excited to see all of you soon bye for now

[Shrini] Hello SAP developers we are happy to announce some major UX announcements on Joule within SAP Build Code. The new design brings a more intuitive user interface which enhances usability and brings in streamlined workflows. Before the update, triggering Joule required navigating through specific entry points like the Guide Center or the Enhance button in the Data Editor. It also meant switching between separate chats for different tasks. With a new design, Joule has a single interface for all functionalities, making it easy to switch between tasks. There are some great new features to make all of this possible. Slash Commands lets you generate a Fiori limits application, get help on SAPUI5 topics, generate a CAP application, add sample data and logic to it. Context variables allows you to use hash symbol in combination with slash commands to reference existing project files. The dynamic suggestion feature shows developers' previous actions automatically. A new file management feature allows you to see which files Joule is using for code generation both during and after the process. A new file list block component allows developers to check the quality of the generated code in the files when multiple files are generated. Custom action buttons allows you to quickly navigate to various interfaces of base code and edit the generated results. If you want to dig deeper into these features, our design lead of SAP Build Code, Ivan, has written this blog post about it. We would love to hear your feedback on these topics.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation 中文用户手册
entry.content:
本文引用并更新了部分来自以下文章的内容， 作者为ArthurYang：

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) 中控台概览及基本操作练习

 开始使用SPA(1):订阅服务,安装本地环境 

开始使用SPA(3):抓取实时汇率信息的开发练习

如果您对BTP感兴趣，BTP个人精选内容目录 | SAP Blogs 可能有更多你需要的内容

 

 

01 SAP Build Process Automation 基本概念及环境准备

02 SAP Build Process Automation 入门学习

03 SAP Build Process Automation 运维管理

 

01 SAP Build Process Automation 基本概念及环境准备

  • 全局账户概览
    全局账户主要具有两个功能：
    a. 创建并编辑子账户，子账户才是真正承载各种服务的租户
    b. 为不同子账户分配资源，例如有人值守机器人给甲账户分配5个license，为乙账户分配10个license
    所以全局账户与日常使用关系不大，要访问机器人平台只需要在子账户内有权限即可

 

 

  • 子账户概览

这里才是管理具体功能权限的地方，可以从全局账户-账户浏览器-(子账户名称)进入

 

 

  • SBPA系统环境准备：云端服务订阅-子账户创建、分配权限及相关操作

SBPA是基于SAP BTP平台构建的，所以我们需要在BTP中控台中为企业的全局账户创建子账户（全局账户在您完成采购之后就已经为您创建好了，子账户则是隔离资源的多租户概念，我们这里针对的是第一次使用BTP的用户）

1.在主控室中 打开 账户浏览器， 点击 创建-子账户

 

2.子账户参数只需要填写名称即可，子域会自动生成，区域(数据中心)与对应的全局账户也会自动填写好

 

 

 

3.接下来为子账户分配全局账户所购买过的License：

在中控台点击 授权-权利分配-选择实体-（选择我们刚刚创建的子账户）

4. 然后点击右侧的 编辑-添加服务计划-搜索process automation-按照采购过的License内容勾选（SBPA服务内尝试全部勾选）-添加服务计划

5. 最后在当前页面找到该服务，修改分配的权限数量，最后点击最顶上的保存即可

6. 为子账户订阅服务：因为分配的License也并不一定要在该子账户上消耗完，所以还需要具体订阅服务去占用 License：
回到 账户浏览器-进入对应子账户-点击左侧的 服务-实例和租用，点击右上角的 创建按钮-选择服务-计划选择租用

7. 分配权限：BTP上的各个服务都有对应的权限，这些权限只有在服务创建成功以后才会生成出来，然后再找到对应成员的权限分配，这个过程十分简单，只需要按照下图操作。

8. 检查：回到 账户浏览器-进入对应子账户-点击左侧的服务-实例和租用，检查是否有SAP Build Process Automation即可，服务名称右侧的按钮即为进入服务主页的链接，推荐将服务主页保存为书签方便进入

SBPA系统环境准备： 本地代理安装

1. 请打开您的SBPA环境（推荐使用最新版的Edge浏览器），进入 控制塔-代理配置-代理更新。第一次下载需要配置技术用户（一次性操作），点击“转到RBSC门户”

2. 创建RBSC用户

 

3. 回到代理更新页面，点击“输入密钥标识”，并输入上一步给出的用户名（注意需要加sap-作为前缀）并粘贴密钥标识。

4. 确认以后点击“转到下载页面”

5. 跟随提示，下载代理本地安装包，允许拓展

6. 安装好机器人和浏览器扩展以后，还需要注册代理方能让BTP识别出装在本地的机器人，复制好SBPA地址。

7. 找到任务栏运行的机器人并按如下操作输入保存，并激活，会弹出浏览器窗口登陆（一次性操作）。

8. 注册好以后回到控制塔代理页面并查看是否已经列出刚刚注册的机器人，接下来还要创建环境来添加代理管理。

9. 回到控制塔，前往环境并开始创建新的环境。环境名称只能使用字母数字字符等.

10. 创建好的环境如图所示，单击进入详情.

11. 在新环境详情页面中，前往代理管理，点击添加代理，选择对应项目并添加。

SBPA系统环境准备： Gui配置 – 激活脚本

1. SAP GUI客户端：激活脚本
打开SAPGUI，并打开其选项
打开：辅助功能与脚本 -> 脚本
选择“启用脚本”，清除其下面的选项

2.SAP服务器端：激活脚本

登录SAP系统，运行事务代码：RZ11
输入参数：sapgui/user_scripting，点“显示”按钮
如果当前值不是TRUE，点“修改值”按钮
输入TRUE，并勾上“在所有的服务器上切换”，点“保存更改”

 

 

02 SAP Build Process Automation 入门学习

SBPA入门学习

SBPA 教程网站：链接

入门教程1：SBPA全功能初步体验

https://developers.sap.com/mission.sap-process-automation.html

入门教程2：RPA部分功能初体验（旧版的UI界面和新版SBPA有所不同，但是功能一致）

https://help.sap.com/docs/IRPA/159a03d61f7647d590ee5c15460311d8/c83d73be6ea2481392a64e0f870fa4 5d.html?locale=en-US

 

SBPA入门学习-创建项目

1. 首先创建一个自动化项目

2. SBPA入门学习-创建自动化

自动化项目创建完成后会自动跳转到项目内，你也可以从SPA主页的前厅进入该项目

第一次打开时会自动提示配置代理版本，会显示该Tenant上注册过的各种代理版本，这里选择我们当前电脑本

地安装的代理版本及对应平台

然后是自动提示创建自动化，如果名称为中文则标识符不会自动变化，创建多个中文名的自动化时注意手动修改标识符

下图为SPA中常用到的所有模块，

RPA功能开发一般只需要用到：

应用程序：教会机器人认识不同的UI页面，一个应用程序即包含对一套系统的识别

自动化：能识别UI页面之后，将各种操作组合成固定的一个个自动化

3. SBPA入门学习-创建应用程序

我们已经创建好了一个自动化，接下来我们教机器人认识一个网页：国家外汇管理局门户网站 http://www.safe.gov.cn/ ，首先在浏览器用新窗口打开该网址

然后在开发窗口内首先创建应用程序（一个应用程序代表一套系统，一套系统可以有多个页面screen，用于教机器人认识页面）

创建-应用程序-填入名称（标识符自动生成）-创建

然后会自动跳转到 添加屏幕 的界面，选择该页面下检测到屏幕，选择国家外汇管理局门户网站web,点击下一步

左侧选择手动捕获（录制器方式将会在其他教程内学

习），右下角点击 捕获（技术代表了该系统前端所用的技术类型，一般都会自动选择）

捕获成功后点击 转到应用程序

4. SBPA入门学习-抓取汇率数据列表

在该应用程序内，我们可以看到之前抓取下来的整个页面组件 和其背后的树结构。

点击屏幕内的汇率价格控件就会显示该组件背后的树结构”UL”，而右侧已经自动尝试了识别，发现我们可以在这个页面里以 “id等于 rmb_query ” 的条件 在整个页面的树状结构中唯一标识该组件。

5. SBPA入门学习-唯一确定汇率数据列表

而我们需要的汇率信息正是这个UL下的每一个LI，但是这些LI都无法自动唯一识别，也没有id，甚至汇率数据每天都会变化，所以我们很难单独识别这些LI

但这些LI的共同点就是他们都在这个UL下，所以我们可以使用 [父亲 等于 [id等于 rmb_query 的UL]] 作为这

一批LI的识别条件，而不是以LI自带的属性作为条件

所以让我们点击其中一个LI，再右键单击父组件UL, 左键点击“添加到条件”

 

但是我们需要的是所有的LI，所以先删除掉LI自动带入的识别条件

 

此时会显示已识别33个元素，

再点击LI 右侧的三个点，点击”设置为集合”，此时即可”声明元素”

 

再点击左侧的已声明元素，去右侧修改它的名称（标识符一旦声明无法修改）

 

6. SBPA入门学习-抓取时政要闻按钮

接下来让我们尝试抓取一个“时政要闻”按钮并在之后进行点击

在屏幕端点击该控件，右边自动挑选了 “Text 等于 时政要闻 的A标签” 作为标识条件，并显示已唯一标识那么我们直接点击 声明元素 即可

接下来点击右上角的保存

 

7. SBPA入门学习-编写自动化

流程的第一步应该是打开这个网页，所以我们进入一开始建立的自动化任务，去右侧搜索 start，将start application拖到主流程中

并单击它，再单击右侧“目标”下的空白框，点选我们之前创建好的应用程序

接下来第二步是读取汇率数据，首先回到右侧自动化详细信息中，把屏幕中我们之前抓取的屏幕拉进主流程

 

由于我们要循环读取很多行，所以我们需要加入一点逻辑，右侧搜索for each，拖到应用程序内

 

单击for each，单击右侧“设置循环列表”下的空之前声明的集合会弹出来，选择它

到这里已经可以遍历集合里的所有元素了，接下来正式抓取文字内容

 

8. SBPA入门学习-初步测试

再搜索get element, 拖进for each内，将get element的目标设置为之前声明的集合
元素的索引设置为2index(此参数即为for each遍历时的index）

接下来我们可以运行一下，保存内容后，在自动化内点击左上角的

再在弹出框中点击测试，即可

 

9. SBPA入门学习-内容保存到Excel

之前只是抓取到了数据，并没有保存，所以我们接下来试一试

右侧搜索 open excel instance, 将该控件拖到Start application之前

再搜索Add Workbook，拖到open excel instance之后， Start application之前

再搜索push value，将该控件拖到for each内的get element控件之后，点击push value，右侧将

其使用的value设置为前一步get element的结果，我这里是4Text

最后将save as workbook控件放在流程结尾前，右侧的filePath设置为C:\Users\Public\test.xlsx

保存整个流程，点击测试

 

10. SBPA入门学习-内容保存到Excel

一切顺利的话，流程图应该如图所示，全过程包括了：

打开一个新的excel，再打开外汇管理局网址

抓取汇率列表，并遍历所有汇率的LI控件，先获取text，再

将text推入当前的excel内最后保存excel

但是目前excel内的汇率数据还只有一整列，每一行是一段长文字（例

如”100人民币64.499林吉特”），我们想把它拆成四小段，来将excel内一整

列的内容分成四段，应该怎么做呢？

方案一是通过数据处理脚本，将Get Element获取到的整段数据拆成四段，再发送给Push Value进行excel的录入

方案二是在抓取的时候就直接去声明LI下面的四个Span，再用两段foreach循环，外层循环遍历所有LI,内层循环遍历四个Span

其他深度用法在这里就不做赘述了，请在blogs.sap.com上搜索内容和这里的深度用法Template

 

03 SAP Build Process Automation 运维管理

1. 机器人管理平台概览
SAP Build Process Automation(也就是这个应用)有两大块，简单理解就是工作流和Process Automation两个功能，我们先关注在Process Automation这部分功能上

2. SBPA运维管理-代理

3. SPA运维管理-为已激活的代理分配自动化流程

首先要明确，自动化内容不会直接和代理相绑定，二者用自动化启动器作为桥梁进行联系，可以暴露给多个代理

连接的方式则是利用 属性 进行匹配，所以我们先为项目创建一个属性，
控制塔-代理属性-创建属性，类型选择预定义，名称填入 测试代理属性，值内填入 测试值1 后回车，即可填入第二个 测试值2，再回车，点击创建

接下来将属性赋予给我们的项目：

在项目内部右上角的设置-属性内，选择我们刚刚创建好的属性和值，应用更改

然后创建自动化启动器，将右侧我们要提供的自动化项目拖入对应启动方式（手动or自动），再保存

然后就可以发布了（右上角的发布按钮），发布是为了固定某个版本的项目内容，所以发布后将无法修改内容，可以在页面最上方切换版本.

发布完成即可部署，部署完成，转到控制塔-环境页面, 选择之前部署的环境，前往自动化启发器，点击配置启动器，选择我们部署好的项目，时间全部选上，创建即可

最后一步是将目标代理与触发器相连：

首先我们需要为已激活的目标代理（等同于目标电脑）添加属性：

控制塔-代理-搜索目标代理--管理属性

然后进入控制塔-环境， 选择部署的环境，前往代理管理-添加代理（这一步骤如已在环境准备做完可忽略）

最后在该电脑上点击本地代理-项目 就可以看到对应的自动化内容了（本地代理插件已选择有人值守模式）

可以点击该内容试一试效果。

 

APPENDIX

1. 我如何注册BTP账户？(SBPA是BTP平台下的一个服务)
答：Link

2. 如果注册完后出现下图提示，或者提示我的账户已注册过但我忘了密码该怎么办？

答：请尝试登录https://awmtxn6rh.accounts.sapcloud.cn/，如果登录成功则说明账号已存在；

如果登陆失败可以通过点击"忘记密码"，如果收到邮件则说明账号已存在，接下来请自行重置密码

entry.title: How to upload, download and delete attachments in the Object Store using SAP Build Apps?
entry.content:
Let's explore an efficient way to create a user-friendly interface for uploading, downloading, and deleting attachments using Object Store in SAP Build Apps.

Prepare the back end

  • Clone the application in the sample project below and deploy it to your cloud foundry space of your BTP Subaccount. https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-objectstore-java-sample (follow the instructions in the README document).

 Tip: Ensure that your Cloud Foundry Space has at least 1 GB Org Quota free for the deployment to succeed.

  • Create a Destination in the BTP Subaccount as below.

Tip 1: As this Destination is referenced in the sample SAP Build apps application (refer section Prepare the Frontend), ensure that the destination Name is exactly the same verbatim ie: ObjectStore.

Tip 2: Add all the Additional Properties as only then this Destination would be discoverable in the SAP Build Apps. Refer to SAP Systems | SAP Help Portal - ‘Adding Data points’ section.

Tip 3: You can find the URL, Client ID, Client Secret and Token Service URL in the ‘Overview’ & ‘Service bindings’ sections in the Cloud Foundry Space corresponding to your deployed application. (NOTE: To access the Cloud Foundry section of your BTP Subaccount, your user must be a member of the Cloud Foundry organization).

Mapping

Parameters

Destination

Application Routes

URL

“clientid”

Client ID

“clientsecret”

Client Secret

“url” plus append in the end /oauth/token

Token service url

Prepare the frontend 

How could an implementation with SAP Build Apps look like?

For inspiration, please check out the ObjectStoreBuildAppsPOC project on github. You can also upload this project directly to your lobby.
Note: this project is a proof of concept created by the author of this GitHub project and is not an official recommendation from SAP; it is provided as-is.

Build and deploy the SAP Build Apps application to the Cloud Foundry space in the same BTP sub account.

Tip: You may also refer to the SAP Build Apps help portal content to learn about Building a SAP Build Apps application and deploying it.

How to use the application end to end?

  • Click on the button ‘New Attachment’.

  • Select the file that must be attached.

  • You may repeat attaching several files by clicking on the button ‘New Attachment’ again.

  • You would be able to see the list of all the uploaded files.

  • Click on the ‘Open File’ option next to each file to download the file and view it.

  • Click on the x option next to each file to delete the file with one click.

  • You can also click on the Overview page of this application to view the meta data such as file size and file type for all the file(s) uploaded (by all the users) to the Object Store of this application.

NOTE: The file Name would not be displayed as it is internally stored as a UUID.

How to customize this app to fit to your needs?

To customize the app to fit to your needs we need to understand the Logic in all the button events (New Attachment, Open File, and x) to some extent.

While most of the Flow functions used in the Logic Canvas are self-explanatory, it's worth noting that we utilize the JavaScript component in the Logic to submit files as formData.

For each button event, I'll provide a brief explanation of the JavaScript component, particularly highlighting areas where customizations can be made.

New Attachment

url

Select the destination (path where the files are uploaded).

 

 

 

 

const url = "destinations/ObjectStore/objectstorage.svc/api/v1/storage/";

 

 

 

 

sizeLimit

Currently supports upload of files up to 5 MB.

 

 

 

 

const sizeLimit = 5*1024*1024;

 

 

 

 

If this must be changed then adjust the code to your preference. For example, to support upload of files up to 10 MB, adjust the code as below:

 

 

 

 

const sizeLimit = 10*1024*1024;

 

 

 

 

Tip: Probably this is the only thing you need to customize in this app.

csrf_token

              There is logic to fetch the csrf-token from the server and appending it to the POST message. This part is required, do not delete it.

Open File

url

Select the destination (path where the files are uploaded).

 

 

 

 

const url = "destinations/ObjectStore/objectstorage.svc/api/v1/storage/";

 

 

 

 

csrf_token

              There is logic to fetch the csrf-token from the server and appending it to the GET message. This part is required, do not delete it.

X (Delete)

url

Select the destination (path where the files are uploaded).

 

 

 

 

const url = "destinations/ObjectStore/objectstorage.svc/api/v1/storage/";

 

 

 

 

csrf_token

              There is logic to fetch the csrf-token from the server and appending it to the GET message. This part is required, do not delete it.

Tip: For all the above button events, one can also think of customizing the Error handling logic in the JS component and overall, in the logic flow of the app if desired.

With this, I hope that you would also be able upload, download and delete attachments in the Object Store using SAP Build Apps. Happy Building!

For any queries here, please reach out to buildapps@sap.com.

Additional reading

Understand CSRF handling in SAP Build Apps - SAP Community

entry.title: How to add a search bar with suggestions in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hello community,

In this blog, I’ll show you how to add a search bar with suggestions to your SAP Build Apps application.

Scenario Overview

Imagine you have an app where you need to search through a list of manufacturers containing over 40,000 entities. Efficiently navigating such a large dataset can be challenging. You want to search using a search bar while also being able to manually browse the list.

Step 1: Creating the Search Component and a Data Variable for the Manufacturers List

Start by creating a component that serves as your search bar, along with a button to open the full list of manufacturers. Here’s how to do it:

  1. Input Field & Icon: Add a simple input field for the search. Next, add an icon (I used journey-arrive from the Fiori icon set) and position it on the right side of the input field. This icon will function as a button to open the complete list of manufacturers.
  2. Data Variable: Create a data variable linked to the data source to retrieve the list of manufacturers.
  3. Text Element: Add a text element that repeats for each manufacturer in your list, stored in a data variable. This list is visible only if your search variable is not empty and the current repeated element does not match the search variable.
    Visibility Formula:
!IS_EMPTY(appVars.selected_manufacturer) && appVars.selected_manufacturer != repeated.current.manufacturerName

Note: Here, selected_manufacturer is stored as an app variable because it's needed outside of this page. However, if it's only required on this page, you can use a page variable instead.

Step 2: Displaying the Manufacturer List

When you click on the search bar icon, it should navigate to a page displaying your manufacturer list. This list is fetched using pagination. If you need help with pagination, refer to this blog on handling OData pagination in SAP AppGyver.

Step 3: Implementing Search Functionality

The search process should be triggered when the variable tied to your custom component changes. You can use the following approach when retrieving your record collection from the backend:

  1. Get record collection: Search with the formula, when a user enters a search term in the search bar, you want to filter the list of manufacturers based on this term. This is achieved through a formula:
{"type":"and","conditions":[{"type":"containsIgnoreCase","property":["manufacturerName"],"value":appVars.selected_manufacturer }]}
  •   type: The filtering method. "containsIgnoreCase" means the search will be case-insensitive and will look for the term anywhere within the property’s value.
  •   property: The field in your data to search within. "manufacturerName" should match the field name in your data variable where manufacturer names are stored.
  •   value: This is the search term, taken from appVars.selected_manufacturer, which holds the user’s input from the search bar.

2. Set Data Variable: Slicing the results. After applying the search filter, limit the results to the first eight records:

SLICE(outputs["Get record collection"].records, 0,8)

Alternative Approaches

Here are some alternative methods and tools that can be used for implementing a search bar with suggestions in SAP Build Apps:

OData Search Capability

  • Description: If your OData service supports a built-in search capability, you can leverage this feature directly without the need for complex filtering logic. For more details, refer to this blog: How to Filter Data in SAP Build Apps Using Record Collection.
  • Advantages: This method simplifies the implementation process since the search is handled by the OData service itself.
  • Limitations: Ensure that your OData service supports search operations. 

Search Bar with Suggestions (Video Tutorial)

  • Description: This video tutorial provides a step-by-step guide on creating a search bar with suggestions in SAP Build Apps: Search bar with suggestions || SAP Build Apps.
  • Advantages: It’s a practical guide for scenarios with a small amount of records.
  • Limitations: This approach may not be suitable for large datasets as it only searches among the first few retrieved records.

Combo Box Component

  • Description: The SAP Build Apps marketplace offers a ready-to-use Combo Box component that provides a dropdown with search capabilities.
  • Advantages: It's a straightforward solution that doesn’t require additional pages or custom components.
  • Limitations: This component might not be ideal if you need to search through very large datasets, as you would need to scroll through a drop-down.

Conclusion

Depending on your specific requirements, you can choose from several approaches to implement a search bar with suggestions in SAP Build Apps. For smaller datasets, you can refer to the video tutorial included in this blog. Alternatively, you can use a pre-built component for simpler implementations or build a custom component to handle larger datasets.

Now, go ahead and build something amazing!

entry.title: Changes to SAP Build Apps UI Canvas
entry.content:
There are 3 key changes to the UI canvas in SAP Build Apps that I have noticed in the last few days. Let me know if you see others, and let me know what you think about the changes.

Change Container's Horizontal / Vertical Layout in the UI Canvas

It used to be that you had to go all the way into a container's Layout tab, then open the Layout area, and then change from horizontal (the default) to vertical.

Now you can do this directly in the UI canvas with 1-click.

This sounds like a small thing, but his was a task done ALL THE TIME for a container, and in any sophisticated UI it was likely you would have dozens of containers. This and the ability to update texts directly in the UI canvas save a lot of time.

 

Component's Core Tab Shuffled

This will actually cost a little time at the beginning but eventually will save ime. The components in the Core tab have been shuffled to make it easier to find the components used most often.

Based on old screenshots, this is what I was able to piece together:

  • Container has been elevated to the very first component.
  • The 1st grouping is now called "Essentials" instead of "Display". Title and Text remain, but are joined with the very common UI elements Button, Input Field and Dropdown Field. (The Card component has been removed completely.)
  • The 2nd grouping is now called "Inputs" instead of "Forms". It includes the remaining components (Slider, Checkbox, Password) along with the elevated Search component.
  • The 3rd group is now called "Display" instead of "Layout". The Scroll View and Row components are now joined by the 3 demoted components from the old Display area: Image, Icon and Spinner. 
  • The 4th and last group, "Lists" is pretty much as it was. All the same components appear, except List Divider has been removed, and the Search Bar has been moved higher up. 

 

Easier to Find Logic You've Written

A common complaint I've heard is that it is difficult to find all the logic you've written. For example, if you are setting the value of a variable and want to change the behavior, it can be hard to find where you added a "Set page variable" flow function.

In a start to help developers, there is now an indicator that tells you whether you have logic associated with a component.

A green dot appears on the UI canvas when you highlight that component, and the same green dot appears next to that component in the tree view.

NOTE: If your logic canvas only has Receive Event flow functions, this is not considered as logic. If you drag other flow functions onto the logic canvas, this is considered having logic even if they are not connected to any event. Also, I do not see any indicator about logic in the main canvas logic area, nor in the variable canvas area.

Leave a comment about what you think about the changes.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 1 - Setup
entry.content:
SAP AI Core is a service in the SAP Business Technology Platform that is designed to handle the execution and operations of your AI assets in a standardized, scalable, and hyperscaler-agnostic way. It provides seamless integration with your SAP solutions. Any AI function can be easily realized using open-source frameworks. SAP AI Core supports full lifecycle management of AI scenarios. Access generative AI capabilities and prompt lifecycle management via the generative AI hub.

SAP Build Apps is a professional application development solution designed for anyone to quickly create apps without code regardless of role or skill level.

In this series of blogs, I will showcase how to integrate SAP AI Core services into SAP Build Apps to develop no-code applications with AI capabilities.

1. Subscribe SAP AI Core service and create instance

refer to https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-launchpad-provisioning.html and https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-generative-ai.html for details on how to setup SAP AI Core service and SAP AI Launchpad.

 

2. Note down the "client-id" and "client-secret" under credentials of your new instance

 

3. Create new deployment in SAP AI Launchpad and note down the url

4. Configure new destination for SAP Build Apps to consume the new AI service deployment

an example of destination configuration is as below

#
#Wed Jul 03 03:57:27 UTC 2024
Type=HTTP
clientId=sb-xxxxxxxxx|xsuaa_std\!xxxxx
clientSecret=xxxxxxxxxxxx
HTML5.DynamicDestination=true
Authentication=OAuth2ClientCredentials
URL.headers.AI-Resource-Group=default
Name=AzureOpenAI
tokenServiceURL=https\://test-int.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
URL=https\://api.ai.prod.eu-central-1.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com/v2/inference/deployments/d123456789c
ProxyType=Internet
tokenServiceURLType=Dedicated
URL.headers.Content-Type=application/json
AppgyverEnabled=true
sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled=true
WebIDEEnabled=true

 

 

After completing the steps mentioned above, our next blog will explore integrating SAP AI Core services into SAP Build Apps to develop no-code applications with AI capabilities.

Link to next blog: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 2 - Integration via Javascript function

entry.title: Summer spark: Inside SAP’s best moments and what’s coming…
entry.content:
As we wrap up the first half of the Inside SAP YouTube series, it's a perfect time to reflect on the insightful sessions we've had so far. Crafted and presented by SAP IT experts themselves, the series has been giving the inside scoop, guaranteeing real, in-depth insights into how SAP operates and its invaluable expertise in Enterprise Architecture, application development on SAP Business Technology Platform, and more.

Kicking off with the Senior Director of SAP Global Experience Technology, Oliver Gutzeit, the session provided a deep dive into the inner workings of SAP's digital landscape. With incredible insights from Christian Freytag and Alexandra Hofmann, we've learned how our digital IT organization supports our business as we evolve our enterprise architecture strategy, the implementation of SAP BTP as well as the SAP Build solutions. Each episode has showcased practical applications, hands-on demos, and innovative solutions, keeping the SAP community engaged and informed. The momentum from these sessions, starting in May, continuing after Sapphire and through the hot summer months, has been truly remarkable.

But wait, there’s more!

We will continue with the series in September, with our Chief Analytics Officer, Pinar Doelen. Throughout the next two sessions, you’ll explore SAP’s data-as-a-product approach, the role of SAP Datasphere, and how AI speeds up insights, as well as learn how SAP is transforming analytics with natural-language experiences in SAP Analytics Cloud.

We want to continue shining a light on SAP’s uniqueness, highlighting the capabilities and possibilities of our technology and solutions. Stay tuned as we return with even more exciting content after the break. In the meantime, you can watch the session replays to catch up on any valuable insights you might have missed. Visit the Inside SAP webpage, register for the series and join our sessions!

See you very soon!

entry.title: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 2 - Integration via Javascript function
entry.content:
If you want maximum flexibility and complete control over your logic flow and don't mind writing your own code, creating a JavaScript function can be an option for integrating SAP AI Core service into your SAP Build Apps application.

1. Configure the BTP destination REST API integration in SAP Build Apps. Unlike integration with the REST API flow function, you don't need to configure additional common request headers and resource schema, as these will be specified in a JavaScript function.

 

 

2. Use the JavaScript flow function to integrate with the new REST API. Note that this integration currently only functions in the deployed app due to the limitations of the destination proxy of SAP Build Apps; it does not work in preview mode. As demonstrated in the sample code below, you will need to manage the CSRF token by first fetching it, saving it, and then using it for POST requests.

sample code of Javascript function

 

let user_input = inputs.user_input;
let system_input = inputs.system_input;
console.log(user_input, system_input);

let text = ''; // Initialize text variable to store the response text

try {
  const svc_url = "destinations/AzureOpenAI/chat/completions?api-version=2024-02-01";

  // Fetch CSRF token from the server
  const csrfResponse = await fetch(svc_url, { method: "GET", headers: { "X-CSRF-Token": "Fetch" } });
  const csrf_token = csrfResponse.headers.get('x-csrf-token'); // Use .get() to retrieve header value

  // Data object for POST request
  const data = {
    messages: [
      { role: "system", content: system_input },
      { role: "user", content: user_input }
    ],
    max_tokens: 1000,
    temperature: 0.5,
    frequency_penalty: 0,
    presence_penalty: 0,
    stop: null // Use null without quotes for actual null value
  };

  // Headers including CSRF token
  const headers = {
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    "ai-resource-group": "default",
    "X-Csrf-Token": csrf_token
  };

  // Fetch request to the service URL
  const fetchResponse = await fetch(svc_url, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: headers,
    body: JSON.stringify(data)
  });

  // Check if the response is ok (status in the range 200-299)
  if (!fetchResponse.ok) {
    throw new Error(`HTTP error! status: ${fetchResponse.status}`);
  }

  // Parse JSON response
  const responseData = await fetchResponse.json();
  console.log(responseData);
  // Assign the response text to the variable
  text = responseData.choices[0].message.content;

} catch (error) {
  // Log any errors that occur during the fetch operation
  console.error(error);
}

// Return the text
  const new_obj = { text };
  console.log(new_obj);
  return new_obj;

 

3. Design your page and UI controls in SAP Build Apps, then open the logic canvas to add logic to the desired UI control, include the JavaScript function in the logic, and add input and output parameters to the JavaScript function. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

4. Deploy your app and run it

Link to previous blog: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 1 - Setup 

Link to next blog: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 3 - Integration via REST API

entry.title: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 3 - Integration via REST API
entry.content:
Another option for integrating SAP AI Core service into your SAP Build Apps application is to use the existing core flow function "Create Record" via BTP destination REST API integration.

1. Configure the BTP destination REST API Integration in SAP Build Apps. You need to configure the additional common request headers and resource schema.

 

2. Design your page and UI controls in SAP Build Apps, then open the logic canvas to add logic to the desired UI control, include the "Create Record" flow function in the logic, and configure the input and output parameters accordingly.

 

3. Use the Create Record flow function to consume the new REST API Integration. Please note that although we use the "Create Record flow function," we are not actually creating any new records in the backend AI service. Instead, we use this flow function to send a "Post" request, allowing the AI service to receive the "prompt" and respond accordingly. After receiving the response from the AI service, we need to further process the response, e.g., parse it and assign variables.

 

 

4. Deploy your app and run it

Link to previous blog: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 2 - Integration via Javascript function

entry.title: Get Started with SAP BTP Document Management Service (video)
entry.content:
Many of you may have been introduced to the SAP BTP Document Management Service in using SAP Build process Automation, which has some integrations with DMS so your process can allow users to upload documents and you can add those documents to approval forms.

DMS also provides other capabilities, such as versioning, search, and access control, and can be used as a content server from the SAP S/4HANA attachment service. @AlexDong has written a wonderful blog collecting a lot of the information about DMS, especially from an SAP S/4HANA perspective.

To help, especially for newbies who've seen DMS from SAP Build perspective, I have created a TechByte video to show what DMS is, the different parts of DMS, how to install the Integration (API) Option, and how to make API calls to work with repos and documents.

In future videos, we'll look at the application option for doing the same thing but with a GUI, how to access documents on mobile, and finally how to work with DMS in SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps.

entry.title: Enable Dropdown List of Users for Manage Members in SAP Lobby
entry.content:
Recently, someone asked me about this message you get when you try to share a project with other users.

It wasn't clear what this was about, but then I learned: instead of entering the users email as text, you can get "value help" or "autocomplete" so you can easily find the user you want to add.

You must first create a system user in your IAS tenant ...

... and create a client ID and secret.

Finally, create a destination called Identity_Authentication_Connectivity_IDS. The URL is in this format:

 

https://<IAS tenant name>.accounts.ondemand.com/scim

 

Use BasicAuthentication, and set the user and password to the client ID and secret from IAS for the new system administrator. (The documentation also explains how to use certificates for the authentication.)

Back in SAP Build lobby, you can select Manage Members for one of the projects, and then start typing a name and you will get autocomplete. Here, I type DJ and see DJ's user, which I can select to share the project with.

You'll also notice that the message we talked about at the start is no longer there.

I did not see a way to select an IAS group. If you know how, let me know. 

  • UPDATE: See my comment below about using custom IDP groups to assign permissions.

😺

entry.title: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 1 - Setup
entry.content:
SAP AI Core is a service in the SAP Business Technology Platform that is designed to handle the execution and operations of your AI assets in a standardized, scalable, and hyperscaler-agnostic way. It provides seamless integration with your SAP solutions. Any AI function can be easily realized using open-source frameworks. SAP AI Core supports full lifecycle management of AI scenarios. Access generative AI capabilities and prompt lifecycle management via the generative AI hub.

The SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) enables developers and technical business users to build multi-channel applications. It allows you to build your application once, in an integrated development environment, and run it natively on mobile devices (Android and iOS) and as a web application (online) in the browser.

In this series of blogs, I will showcase how to integrate SAP AI Core services into SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) to develop mobile applications with AI capabilities.

  • Part 1: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup (Current Blog)
  • Part 2: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Business Use Cases 
  • Part 3: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Measurement Readings

 

SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup

1. Subscribe SAP AI Core service and create instance

Please refer to SAP tutorials:

Use Boosters for Free Tier Use of SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

Prompt LLMs in the generative AI hub in SAP AI Core & Launchpad 

for details on how to setup SAP AI Core service and SAP AI Launchpad.

2. Note down the "client-id" and "client-secret" under credentials of your new instance

3. Create new deployment in SAP AI Launchpad and note down the url

4. Configure new destination for SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) Web Client to consume the new AI service deployment

If you want to test and run your app in SAP Business Application Studio (MDK Editor) and deploy it as a web app running on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), you need to configure the BTP destination for your AI service deployment of SAP AI Core services.

an example of destination configuration is as below

#
#Wed Jul 03 03:57:27 UTC 2024
Type=HTTP
clientId=sb-xxxxxxxxx|xsuaa_std\!xxxxx
clientSecret=xxxxxxxxxxxx
HTML5.DynamicDestination=true
Authentication=OAuth2ClientCredentials
URL.headers.AI-Resource-Group=default
Name=AzureOpenAI
tokenServiceURL=https\://test-int.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
URL=https\://api.ai.prod.eu-central-1.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com/v2/inference/deployments/d123456789c
ProxyType=Internet
tokenServiceURLType=Dedicated
URL.headers.Content-Type=application/json
WebIDEEnabled=true

5. Configure your SAP Mobile Service instance to consume the new AI service deployment

You can create a new mobile service instance or modify your existing instance to add new mobile connectivity for your AI service deployment on SAP AI Core services.

6. Setup your MDK development and testing environment. 

Download and install the SAP Mobile Services Client on your mobile devices, or build your Mobile Development Kit Client using the MDK SDK, and create a new MDK project in Business Application Studio (MDK Editor) as usual.

If you are new to MDK, please check out the following tutorials

Create Your First MDK Application

Build Your Mobile Development Kit Client Using MDK SDK 

After completing the steps mentioned above, our next blog will explore integrating SAP AI Core services into SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) to develop mobile applications with AI capabilities.

Next blog: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 2 – Business Use Cases

entry.title: Condition-based exception handling for process instances through “Process Visibility” Actions
entry.content:
Introduction:

In this Blog, I would like to explain how  

  • Exceptions can be handled in your processes using Process Visibility actions, based on conditions.  

  • The triggering of processes can be enabled based on visibility insights, to receive notifications and react to critical situations. 

This Blog aims to provide information about the configuration steps to leverage the insight to action workflows to notify administrators and/or business users when certain conditions arise in a business process. 

 

Process flow - Setup and Configuration:

Subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation as documented here. 

Configure SAP Build Process Automation Destination as described in the Prerequisites section towards the end of this Blog.

 

Use case:

The Sales Order Management scenario is used to illustrate this Blog. Based on sales order data received as input, the approval process is optimised and happens manually or automatically. In case the process fails, some actors can be notified, as Administrator or Business Expert, via a trigger capability in Process Visibility. 

See below, a collapsed view of the process model:

 

Process Model

So, in this Sales Order Management Project, we have an Order Processing Demo process (as shown above). The project for Sales Order Management contains several artifacts including a process, an automation and a visibility scenario. The process model Order Processing Demo has a Form trigger. The user will enter a SalesOrderID and submit. An automation is then executed to extract the Sales Order details (sales order amount, etc.) from an excel document based on the SalesOrderID. Manual or automatic approval is evaluated into a condition and recipients receive a notification. 

There is also a Process Visibility scenario to notify Administrators or Business Users when a State (i.e. abruptly ended), a Status (i.e. critical) or SubStatus (i.e. failed) is reached. There are standard States, Status and SubStatus and it is also possible to create custom ones. It is possible to define a System trigger (automatic trigger) or User action trigger (manual trigger) in the Process Visibility scenario and configure the workflow or process that will be started if the condition is met (i.e. Process SubStatus equals failed). 

Please see the help documentation for more details. 

In our use case, we have modelled a Visibility scenario with an action (Trigger type: System) to trigger a workflow (NotifyProcessAdministrators) on failed or suspended instances.

 

Let’s get into the configuration details.. 

So, within the Visibility scenario, firstly appropriate attribute needs to be created. This is required, as the context needs to be passed, when the workflow from Process Visibility is to be triggered. Within the context, two values are passed. 

  • SubStatus – This is a default Attribute from Process Visibility, to provide insights on a process state.  

 

Details about the SubStatus are available in the 'Status' tab.  

 Status

  • workflowInstanceID – This is a Calculated Attribute that needs to be created. In this case, please note that the Name needs to be the same as used in the workflow start payload. 

This information will be used (for example), in the Task title sent to the administrator.  

Attribute_1

 

Attribute_2

As a next step, a destination with configuration OAuth2ClientCredentials would be required to start an instance of the workflow. See Prerequisites section for destination configuration. This destination will be used later in the configuration. It is meant to initiate the workflow. 

To demo the feature, we are using an existing workflow model, available at SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This workflow can be consumed from Process Visibility scenario with minimal configuration. It is triggered when the Process Status changes to any of the defined/custom statuses configured in the scenario. In our case, as our actions include ‘suspended’/’failed’, a workflow should be triggered when these conditions are met. 

Below, an overview of the model opened within SAP Business Application Studio. When the SubStatus condition is reached and evaluated, the recipient will receive a user task to process. 

Note: A group within SAP BTP for Process Admin / Process Operator needs to be available in the SAP BTP Cockpit and configured with users. If the group is not available as task recipient, then assign the task manually from the monitoring application. 

 Workflow Model

 

Then, select the 'Actions' tab to create an action. You can either trigger a workflow or a process. We are calling a Workflow in our case with the below configuration. 

 

Actions

 

'Conditions' section would be shown once action is created. You will have the option to create certain conditions to Indicate when the Workflow will be triggered. Here, we have defined the conditions (Process Failed / Process Suspended).  

Select the relevant trigger type: in our case ‘System’ 

Select the Environment variable that was created in the project.  

Workflow definition: You can select a workflow or a process. Here we select a workflow as we have already deployed this model in our tenant.  

Start context: Pass the name of the calculated attribute “workflowInstanceId” that was created in the earlier step.

Add Conditions using ‘Status’ and ‘SubStatus’ attributes.

'Save' and 'activate' your Visibility Scenario. 

 

At runtime, on SubStatus change, Process Visibility will trigger the System action and will start the workflow. A workflow task will be available in the inbox of the recipient group. 

During the workflow execution at runtime, if an unforeseen situation occurs, Process Visibility will start the workflow definition “NotifyProcessAdministrators” to notify the recipients, that the process requires attention. In our scenario, the recipients will receive a task with details about the instance and the latest error message. The workflow model is fully customisable.

 My Inbox 

 The instances of the notifications workflows are visible in SAP Build Monitoring section. 

 

Logs

 

The context data of the instance is also visible and contains information about the failed process. The administrator can navigate to the failed instance to analyse the logs in detail and take necessary steps for resolution of the issue. 

 Context

Note: Ensure that you regenerate the scenario data in the Monitoring section (Path: Monitoring --> Visibility Scenarios --> <Your_Visibility_Scenario> --> Processing Information --> Clear Processed Data. Once the data is cleared, click on ‘Process Data’ 

 

Prerequisites:

  • HTTP destination is required in the BTP subaccount where SAP Build Process Automation is subscribed. Create a destination with the name “sap_process_automation_service” and with the following configuration, if it doesn’t exist already. Please refer to this documentation to understand how HTTP destination is created.  

Destination1

 

  • Destination with the Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials needs to be configured to start an instance of the workflow. More configuration details of the destination be found here. 

 

Destination2

Recap:

Trigger Process -> Needs no destination. This is only available in SAP Build Process Automation and not in Workflow Management. 

Trigger Workflow -> Is offered both in SAP Build Process Automation and Workflow Management. 

For Workflow Management, please refer to this document on how the destination should be created.  

For SAP Build Process Automation, please refer this documentation. 

 

Should you have any questions, you can comment below or post a question to our SAP Build Process Automation forum. Check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to know more about upcoming features.

Until the next blog post, stay tuned!

entry.title: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 2 – Business Use Cases
entry.content:
Once SAP AI Core services are integrated into MDK (See Part 1), there will be numerous possibilities for MDK developers to implement AI-related use cases for end users. Let me introduce one such use case: generating structured data from multimodal inputs such as text, voice, and image using Generative AI. This just serves as a small step into a new universe of AI capabilities for MDK developers.

In this series of blogs, I will showcase how to integrate SAP AI Core services into SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) to develop mobile applications with AI capabilities.

  • Part 1: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup
  • Part 2: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Business Use Cases (Current Blog)
  • Part 3: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Measurement Readings

SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services – Business Use Cases

Enable Multimodality AI in MDK: Incorporating multimodal AI in mobile development with MDK allows for integration of various input methods, such as free text input, photo capture, and audio and video recording. Following are a few potential scenarios:

  • Measurement readings: Capture and record correct measurements.
  • Anomaly detection:  Detect and report anomalies/irregularities in real time.
  • Maintenance guidelines generation: Automatically generate maintenance guidelines based on gathered data.
  • Work order and operation recording: Efficiently record and track work orders and operation.

To enable multimodality AI in app development using MDK, we can incorporate various input methods such as free text input, photo capture, audio and video recording. This allows users to interact with the app in a more natural and versatile manner. For instance, technicians can take measurement readings directly within the app, which can then be analyzed for anomaly detection. This helps in identifying potential issues before they escalate. Additionally, the app can generate maintenance guidelines based on the data collected, ensuring users have access to up-to-date and relevant information. Furthermore, the app can facilitate the recording of work orders and operations, streamlining the workflow and ensuring that all activities are documented accurately. By  enabling these AI capabilities, we can create a comprehensive and efficient solution that leverages the power of multimodality AI to enhance app functionality and user experience.

 

Use Case – Meter Reading and Equipment Maintenance

1. Use camera to take meter reading.

2. AI to detect anomaly with camera and predict maintenance.

3. AI to generate service operation to-do list

 

Use Case - Record Work Order and Operation

1. Use voice or free text to input plain text.

2. Save the input as unstructured data

3. Conduct a similarity search in the vector database to construct a prompt.

 

4. Send the prompt to a foundational LLM model to generate structured information.

 

5. Populate the generated information into a form and confirm the data transaction.

 

The architectural diagram and workflow of these use cases are shown below.

 

Step-by-step flow in the above workflow:

Step 1: begins with the MDK developer to enable SAP AI core services, as described in the previous blog

Step 2 and 3: involve using a Retrieval Argument Generation (RAG) embedding model, such as text-embedding-ada-002, which is optional for general use, to embed and index domain-specific knowledge of your use case. This includes input text chunking, creation of vector embeddings, and storing and deletion of the vector embeddings in the Embedding Storage of the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine.
